description: Drive a WLED LED strip from video content playing in Jellyfin
name: WLED TV
overview: Samples pixel colours along the edges of the playing video and sends them to a WLED controller in real time, creating an immersive edge-lighting effect that reacts to the content on screen.

versions.changelog:
Auto-detect letterbox/pillarbox bars.

Scans the current frame at 64x64 resolution every 2 s to find the edges of the actual picture area, then restricts all LED colour sampling to that area. Handles static bars and dynamic aspect-ratio changes mid-video.

Reduce unexpected-close retry from 2s to 200ms; add ws error/close code logging.

While the root cause (mock closing connections after color data) is investigated separately, this makes the visible gap much shorter. The close code logged in DevTools will reveal exactly why the browser is closing.

Fix: LEDs flicker every 2 seconds instead of updating continuously.

- Stop is now debounced: requires 3 consecutive ticks (~300ms) with no video before stopping, tolerating HLS segment boundaries
- Deliberate closes (stop → closeWs) no longer impose the 2-second reconnect penalty
- Video lookup now also checks same-origin iframes (Jellyfin ViewManager)

Complete rewrite of the injected sampling script.

Clean-room architecture replacing 5 layers of patches:
- Loop: running flag + clearTimeout, no loopGen counter
- WebSocket: ensureWs() called every tick unconditionally; wsRetryAt timestamp replaces wsReconnecting flag
- Sampling: readyState >= 2 guard retained; poll() is start-only

Fix: LEDs frozen during HLS startup / buffering.

openWebSocket was only called from inside the readyState >= 2 guard, so during
HLS startup (readyState=1, dimensions known but no decoded frame) the WebSocket
was never re-established after it closed. LEDs showed the last frame from the
detail-page preview and never updated during actual playback.

Fix: openWebSocket is now called unconditionally on every sampleStep tick.

Fix: HLS playback crash caused by sampling before video has frame data.

Calling ctx.drawImage(video) while video.readyState < 2 (before the first
frame is decoded) triggered an hls.js internalException, causing Jellyfin
to stop playback immediately. Added video.readyState >= 2 guard.

Diagnostic release: adds console logging + fixes checkState over-stopping.

Open browser DevTools console while playing a video to see what is happening.
Key log lines: loop started/stopped, ws opened/closed, frame drop reasons.

Fix: LEDs stop updating after a handful of frames

The WebSocket onclose handler captured the 'ws' variable by reference.
If the old socket closed after a new connection was already assigned to
'ws', the old handler fired 'ws = null', killing the new socket. The
sampling loop kept running but all frames were silently dropped.

Fix: guard the null-out with a local socket reference (ws === socket).

Fix: LEDs only updating once (loop stopping permanently)

video.videoWidth can be 0 during buffering even while not paused.
The loop stopped itself and checkState could not restart it because
its own condition also requires videoWidth > 0. The loop now stays
alive through transient states, only stopping when the video element
is fully removed.
Fix: config URL field lookup used old element ID (txtWledUrl) instead of txtWledWsUrl, causing null dereference on every load and save.
Switch colour stream to direct WebSocket (browser to WLED)
Fix CW/CCW direction; fix LEDs staying on after stop; improve off payload

Strip direction; stall watchdog; frame pacing; off on stop

- LEDs/m removed; strip start and direction are now separate settings
- Counter-clockwise direction support (default); fixes mirrored output
- Sequential sampling loop with backpressure (waits for POST to complete)
- 5-second stall watchdog restarts the loop if a request is dropped
- Frame pacing: delay is reduced by time already spent sampling/sending
- LEDs turn off when returning to the Jellyfin interface; stay on when paused
- Config page now saves and loads correctly

Fix: config page completely unresponsive

The script tag was outside the data-role=page div so Jellyfin never
executed it. Moved inside the div. Also sets correct default values
on the LEDs/m (60) and update interval (100ms) inputs.

Fix: config page not responding to any interactions

All DOM lookups and event listeners were running before the page was
mounted, causing silent failures. Moved inside the pageshow handler.
